author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
virajāra pāre, śuddhaparavyoma⋅dhāma
tadupari śrī⋅gokula⋅‘vṛndāraṇya’⋅nāma

vṛndāvana cintāmaṇi, cidānanda⋅ratnakhani,
cinmaya apūrva⋅daraśana
ta̐hi mājhe camatkāra, kṛṣṇa vanaspati⋅sāra,
nīlamaṇi tamāla jemana

tā̐he eka svarṇamayī, latā sarvadhāma⋅jayī,
uṭhiyāche paramapāvanī
hlādinī⋅śaktira sāra, ‘mahābhāva’ nāma jā’ra,
tribhuvanamohana⋅mohinī

‘rādhā’⋅nāme paricita, tuṣiyā govinda⋅cita,
virājaye parama⋅ānande
sei latā⋅patra⋅phula, lalitādi sakhīkula,
sabe mili’ vṛkṣe dṛḍha bāndhe

latāra paraśe praphulla tamāla
latā chāḍi’ nāhi rahe konakāla

tamāla chāḍiyā latā nāhi bā̐ce
se latā⋅milana sadā-kāla jāce

bhakativinoda milana dohāra
nā cāhe kakhana vinā kichu āra
en_translation:
On the other side of Virajā river, is the pure transcendental abode. Above that is Śrī Gokula, named the forest of Vṛndāvana.

Vṛndāvana is a Cintāmaṇi, a mine of transcendental bliss jewels. It is a spiritual, unparalleled vision. Within it is a wonder: Kṛṣṇa is the essence of all trees, like a sapphire-like Tamāla tree.

Upon Him is a golden creeper, conquering all abodes, risen, supremely purifying. She is the essence of the bliss-giving potency, whose name is Mahābhāva (the highest state of ecstatic love). She is enchanting even the enchanter of the three worlds.

Known by the name Rādhā, pleasing the heart of Govinda, she shines in supreme bliss. That creeper's leaves and flowers, the group of confidantes like Lalitā, all together, firmly bind the tree.

By the touch of the creeper, the Kṛṣṇa tree is joyful. Without the creeper, it cannot exist at any time.

Without the Tamāla, the creeper cannot survive. That creeper always yearns for union.

Bhaktivinoda sees their union, and desires nothing else at any time.
